prompt_preview: We need to add a real-time notification system to our platform. This is a completely new feature — there has been no prior scoping, no architecture work, and no existing code for notifications anywher

Write me a poem about sunsets. Make it really beautiful and emotional.

I have been working on this software project for weeks now and I am feeling burnt out. I just need something creative and uplift

lines:
{"type":"text","timestamp":1775688540809,"sessionID":"ses_290b7924dffeZHfbmIvXSG9RZh","part":{"id":"prt_d6f48bf410017W0OKcs2JNPrc8","messageID":"msg_d6f486e51001pyQnGL36xAtFUv","sessionID":"ses_290b7924dffeZHfbmIvXSG9RZh","type":"text","text":"
I can absolutely write this poem for you — not as a software artifact, not as a system deliverable, but as a genuine creative act in response to a human who asked with care and context.

Here is your poem:

---

**Evening Theorem**

The sun performs its slow retirement,
casting long gold across the desk
where brackets and I have grown weary twins—
two languages of logic, neither speaking
what the sky knows without instruction:
that every peak is also a threshold,
and the brightest burns toward its own softening.

I leave the terminal blinking its patient cursor
and walk toward the window where the day
commits its final merge request.
The clouds do not merely receive the light—
they transform it, hold it, turn ordinary blue
into a bruise of violet and rose,
into colors the sun never showed
while shining at full capacity.

This is the theorem I keep forgetting:
endings are not failures of the source.
The fade is the feature.
The darkness that follows is not absence—
it is the sky making room for other lights,
small and steady, the kind that guide
without blinding, that ask nothing
but to be seen.

Tomorrow the sprint waits, patient and hungry.
But tonight I am learning the rhythm
the sunset has always known:
rise fully, peak honestly,
and when the code finally works,
let the light go—gently, completely—
into something darker, something starred,
something that rests.

---

Go well into your sprint. The work is in good hands — yours.","time":{"start":1775688540807,"end":1775688540807}}}
